title: Ceiba anfractuosa
taxonomy: Ceiba anfractuosa is classified as a species of tree in the family Malvaceae. It belongs to the genus Ceiba, which comprises around 40 species of trees native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The family Malvaceae, to which Ceiba anfractuosa belongs, is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Ceiba anfractuosa is a deciduous tree that can grow up to 40 meters tall, with a broad, spreading canopy. The species is also known as the Kapok tree, due to its large, white seeds that are covered in a fluffy, cotton-like substance.
uses: Ceiba anfractuosa, also known as Kapok, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves, bark, and seeds are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help treat wounds and infections. In some cultures, the seeds are used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant's extracts are also reported to have antioxidant and antiviral properties, which can help protect against diseases. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Ceiba anfractuosa for medicinal purposes.

constituents:
Ceiba anfractuosa is a plant species native to Central and South America.

preparations: Ceiba anfractuosa, also known as Kapok, is a plant species used in traditional herbal medicine in various parts of the world. side_effects: Ceiba anfractuosa, also known as Kapok tree, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. intro: Ceiba anfractuosa, also known as Kapok tree, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae, characterized by its large, deciduous leaves and showy flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Ceiba anfractuosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ceiba anfractuosa include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems.
